issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `` element at line 133 in the hero image section lacks an alt attribute, which prevents screen readers from describing the visual content to users with visual impairments. This is a critical failure under WCAG 1.1.1 (Non-text Content).
issuesFound.fix: Line 133: ``

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on button (toggle button missing aria-expanded)
issuesFound.explanation: The button at line 108 (nav-toggle) controls expandable content (via aria-controls="nav-menu") but lacks aria-expanded, violating SC 4.1.2 (Scope of Onscreen Content). This prevents keyboard users from knowing whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 108: `<button type="button" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="nav-menu" aria-label="Open navigation menu" class="nav-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple links (lines 105, 109, etc.) use generic text like "Products," "Solutions," "Resources," "Pricing," "About us," "Contact," "Start free trial," "Watch the demo," etc., which are insufficiently descriptive for screen readers. These links fail WCAG 2.4.4 (Link Purpose) as they lack meaningful context.
issuesFound.fix: Replace generic text with more descriptive alternatives where possible (e.g., "View all products" for "Products" link). For example: Line 105: `View all product offerings` Line 109: `Accessibility solutions for design teams` (for Solutions dropdown)

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference: element references id that does not exist
issuesFound.explanation: The `<button>` at line 108 references `nav-menu` via `aria-controls="nav-menu"`, but no element with id `nav-menu` exists in the document. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Scope of Onscreen Content).
issuesFound.fix: Line 108: `<button type="button" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="menu-content" aria-label="Open navigation menu" class="nav-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: Heading level skip
issuesFound.explanation: The heading hierarchy skips from `<h1>` (hero heading) to `<h2>` (features section) without a proper intermediate heading. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 102: `

Feature Overview

`

issuesFound.title: Missing skip link to main content
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link to main content is present (`Skip to main content`), but the skip link to navigation is missing a corresponding skip link to the main navigation section (`#main-nav`). The skip link to search is also present (`Skip to search`).

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-labelledby for interactive elements with ARIA controls
issuesFound.fix:
The buttons with `aria-controls` should have `aria-labelledby` if they are toggling content with a heading or label. For example, the navigation toggle button should reference the heading for the navigation menu.

  **Correction:** The navigation toggle button does not need `aria-labelledby` since it's not tied to a heading. However, the dropdown menus should have proper labels for better accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="button" for interactive elements
issuesFound.fix: None of the buttons explicitly have `role="button"` (which is redundant since `<button>` already has this role). This is not a failure but a best practice.

issuesFound.title: Missing heading for FAQ accordion
issuesFound.fix:
The FAQ section lacks a heading. Add `

Frequently asked questions

` before the FAQ list.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="list" for filter tabs
issuesFound.fix: The filter tabs should have `role="list"` to indicate they are a list of interactive items. Add `role="list"` to `<ul role="list" class="filter-tabs" aria-label="Product category filter">`.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="list" for testimonials grid
issuesFound.fix: The testimonials grid should have `role="list"` to indicate it is a list of testimonial items. Add `role="list"` to `<ul role="list" class="testimonials-grid">`.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="region" for status region
issuesFound.fix: The status region should have `role="region"` to indicate it is a live region. Add `role="region"` to `<div aria-live="polite" aria-atomic="true" class="sr-only" id="status-region">`.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="search" for search form
issuesFound.fix: The search form should have `role="search"` to indicate it is a search form. Add `role="search"` to `<form id="site-search" role="search" action="/search" method="get">`.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="listbox" for filter tabs
issuesFound.fix: The filter tabs should have `role="listbox"` to indicate they are a listbox with interactive items. Add `role="listbox"` to `<div role="group" aria-labelledby="filter-label">`.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="group" for pricing toggle
issuesFound.fix: The pricing toggle should have `role="group"` to indicate it is a group of interactive items. Add `role="group"` to `<div class="pricing-toggle" role="group" aria-labelledby="billing-toggle-label">`.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="application" for footer
issuesFound.fix: The footer should have `role="application"` to indicate it is the main application footer. Add `role="application"` to `<footer role="contentinfo">`.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="main" for main content
issuesFound.fix: The main content should have `role="main"` to indicate it is the main content area. Add `role="main"` to `<main id="main-content">`.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="navigation" for footer navigation
issuesFound.fix: The footer navigation sections should have `role="navigation"` to indicate they are navigation areas. Add `role="navigation"` to each `<nav>` in the footer.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="status" for footer bottom
issuesFound.fix: The footer bottom section should have `role="status"` to indicate it is a status area. Add `role="status"` to `<div class="footer-bottom">`.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="dialog" for cookie settings button
issuesFound.fix: The cookie settings button should have `role="dialog"` if it opens a cookie consent dialog. Add `role="dialog"` to `Cookie settings` if a dialog is intended.
